label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure this out. The question is asking whether following a series of steps will bring you back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 5 steps backward, then 8 steps forward, then 7 steps forward, then 5 steps backward. The options are Yes or No. Let me break it down step by step. First, always facing forward. So, when the instructions say "take 5 steps backward," you're facing forward but stepping backward. Similarly, when taking steps forward, you're moving in the direction you're facing. Let me track the position relative to the starting point. Let's assume the starting position is 0. 1. Start at position 0. 2. Take 5 steps backward: since you're facing forward, stepping backward means moving in the negative direction. So your new position is 0 - 5 = -5. 3. Take 8 steps forward: moving in the positive direction from -5. New position: -5 + 8 = 3. 4. Take 7 steps forward: from 3, moving 7 steps forward is 3 + 7 = 10. 5. Take 5 steps backward: from 10, moving backward 5 steps is 10 - 5 = 5. After all these steps, you end up at position 5, which is not the starting point (position 0). Therefore, you do not return to the starting
